claim: This text contains no substantive scientific claims; it consists solely of author affiliations and contact information.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products: A Computational Approach to Targeting AgeRelated Glycative Damage (Stage 1 Registered Report)

claim: The top 100 -200 computational designs will be validated experimentally through yeast surface display screening against CML-modified bovine serum albumin, and top candidates will undergo binding affinity characterization by biolayer interferometry.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: We hypothesize that this pipeline will yield at least one binder with measurable binding to CML-modified protein and sub-micromolar affinity.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: This work represents the first application of RFdiffusion All-Atom to AGE targeting and will establish whether computationally designed protein binders can serve as a new class of anti-glycation therapeutics.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: AGE cross-links increase tissue stiffness and reduce turnover in long-lived extracellular matrix proteins like collagen.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance

claim: None have achieved regulatory approval for AGE-related indications.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance

claim: Aminoguanidine reached Phase III trials but was discontinued due to adverse effects.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance

claim: No group has yet applied this technology to AGE targeting.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance

claim: This project proposes a novel strategy: designing de novo protein binders that recognize and sequester specific AGE adducts.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance

claim: Unlike small molecules, designed proteins can achieve high affinity and specificity through extensive complementary surfaces.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance

claim: This approach has been validated for other small molecule targets including digoxigenin and heme.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance

claim: A designed AGE binder could function therapeutically by intercepting circulating AGEs before they engage RAGE or cross-link tissue proteins.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance

claim: Such binders would serve as research tools for detecting and quantifying specific AGE species, and as scaffolds for downstream enzyme design to actively degrade AGEs.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance

claim: This study will test three preregistered hypotheses: H1: RFdiffusion All-Atom can generate protein scaffolds with designed binding pockets for CML (the most abundant AGE species).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: H2: At least one computationally designed binder will show measurable binding to CMLmodified bovine serum albumin (AGE-BSA) in yeast surface display screening, defined as a signal-to-background ratio greater than 3.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: H3: Top binder candidates will exhibit sub-micromolar affinity for AGE-BSA as measured by biolayer interferometry (BLI).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: H1 is the primary outcome as it establishes the foundational feasibility of the computational approach.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: H2 constitutes the primary experimental outcome, since yeast display screening is the initial gate for identifying active binders.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: H3 is a secondary outcome that characterizes the quality of candidates passing the primary screen.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: In the event no binding is detected in the initial yeast display screen, contingent analyses are pre-specified in the Methods section.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: The study will be completed over 6 months following Stage 1 peer review approval.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: A Stage 2 submission is anticipated approximately 8 months after Stage 1 acceptance, allowing time for data analysis and manuscript preparation.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: The Methods section provides sufficient detail to allow full replication of all computational and experimental procedures.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Carboxymethyllysine (CML) will serve as the primary design target.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: CML is the most abundant AGE in human tissues, is chemically stable, has commercially available standards, and its structure is well characterized.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: The molecular structure of CML bound to a lysine backbone will be used as the design input.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: The study design follows a three-stage workflow: backbone generation using RFdiffusion All-Atom, sequence design using ProteinMPNN, and in silico filtering using AlphaFold2 and Rosetta to advance top designs to experimental testing.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: A total of 10,000 backbone designs will be generated conditioned on the CML structure to produce diverse binding pocket geometries.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Five independent sequences will be generated per backbone, optimized for the predicted binding interface.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Designs will be filtered based on structural confidence (pLDDT > 85) and Rosetta binding energy estimation, with the top 100-200 designs advancing to experimental testing.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Designed binders will be displayed on yeast cell surfaces as Aga2p fusions.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: A pooled library of top designs will be constructed through oligonucleotide synthesis and Gibson assembly.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: The library will be sorted against biotinylated AGE-BSA using fluorescence-activated cell sorting (FACS).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Binding cells will be enriched over 2 -3 rounds of selection.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Individual clones from the enriched population will be sequenced by Sanger sequencing and characterized.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Binding kinetics (kon, koff) and equilibrium affinity (KD) will be measured using biolayer interferometry (BLI) on an Octet RED96 instrument (Sartorius) with AGE-BSA immobilized on streptavidin biosensors.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Specificity will be assessed by parallel testing against unmodified BSA at equivalent concentrations.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: No exclusion criteria apply to the computational designs; all designs passing the pre-specified in silico filtering thresholds will advance to experimental testing.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: This estimate is conservative given that AGE-BSA presents multiple CML epitopes per molecule, increasing the effective target valency and concentration relative to single-epitope targets used in prior benchmarks.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: The minimum detectable effect is defined operationally: a hit rate of ≥ 1% (at least 1 binder out of 100 tested) would be considered evidence that the computational pipeline can identify functional binders, consistent with first-in-class platform development.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Data will be collected from four sources: (i) RFdiffusion All-Atom output files (PDB format) for backbone designs; (ii) AlphaFold2 pLDDT scores and predicted aligned error (PAE) matrices; (iii) Rosetta binding energy scores; and (iv) BLI sensorgrams from the Octet RED96.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Data Collection, Processing, and Planned Analysis Data Sources and Instruments

claim: FACS data will be collected on a BD FACSAria instrument and analyzed using FlowJo (v10).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Data Collection, Processing, and Planned Analysis Data Sources and Instruments

claim: All raw data files will be retained in their original formats.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Data Collection, Processing, and Planned Analysis Data Sources and Instruments

claim: Computational designs will be filtered by the following sequential pre-processing criteria prior to experimental testing: (1) pLDDT > 85 across the full design; (2) predicted TM-score to design model > 0.8; (3) no steric clashes with CML ligand as assessed by Rosetta fa_rep term < 5.0. Designs failing any criterion are excluded from experimental testing.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: BLI sensorgrams will be double-referenced (reference channel and buffer-only reference) prior to kinetic fitting using the Octet Analysis Studio software (Sartorius, v12).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: The primary outcome is the fraction of designs showing a binding signal above background in yeast surface display (hit rate).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Designs with a signal-to-background ratio > 3 relative to noligand controls will be classified as hits.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: The hit rate and its 95% Wilson confidence interval will be reported.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Correlation between in silico metrics (AlphaFold2 pLDDT, Rosetta binding energy) and experimental binding, assessed by Spearman rank correlation.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Structural diversity of successful binders, assessed by pairwise backbone RMSD clustering.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Affinity distribution of BLI-validated hits, reported as KD values with 95% confidence intervals from curve fitting.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: No imputation will be applied to missing BLI measurements; any binder for which a reliable fit cannot be obtained will be reported as ' fit failed ' with the raw data provided.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: AlphaFold2 predicts high confidence (pLDDT > 85) for 15 -20% of designs after ProteinMPNN sequence assignment, consistent with published benchmarks for the method.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: No wet laboratory pilot data has been generated, which is appropriate given the exploratory nature of this project and the absence of prior art applying RFdiffusion All-Atom to AGE targets.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Secondary analyses are exploratory; no correction for multiple comparisons will be applied, and all p-values will be reported without adjustment with a note that these analyses are hypothesis-generating.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Designs for which BLI fitting fails (R ² < 0.95) will be excluded from affinity analyses but reported descriptively.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Outliers in BLI data will be defined as measurements deviating more than 3 standard deviations from the replicate mean and will be excluded after documentation.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Affinity values (KD) will be reported as mean ± standard deviation from triplicate measurements.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: The study utilizes RFdiffusion All-Atom, ProteinMPNN, AlphaFold2 v2.3, Rosetta 3.13, FlowJo v10, Octet Analysis Studio v12, and Python 3.11 with specific library versions for its computational and analytical workflows.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: If no designs show a signal-to-background ratio > 3 in the initial yeast display screen, then the following pre-specified contingent analyses will be executed: testing binding to free CMLlysine conjugate to determine whether the absence of binding reflects a scaffold problem or a target accessibility problem, and assessing whether designs adopt the computationally predicted fold using circular dichroism spectroscopy to determine whether folding failures explain the absence of binding.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: If H2 and H3 are supported (binding detected with sub-micromolar affinity), this will establish proof-of-concept for a new class of AGE-targeting protein therapeutics and produce binder sequences that can be optimized through directed evolution.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: This would represent the first demonstration that computationally designed proteins can specifically engage a posttranslational glycation modification, advancing both the therapeutic and research tool landscape for aging biology.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: If binding is not achieved (H2 not supported), the negative results will inform the field about the challenges of designing binders for post-translationally modified amino acids and guide future computational approaches.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Results from the contingent analyses will distinguish between scaffold folding failures and target accessibility as limiting factors, providing actionable direction for subsequent design iterations.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Either outcome has direct implications for the feasibility of protein-based anti-AGE interventions.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: These findings will contribute to the literature on de novo protein design benchmarks and to the aging intervention field.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Successful development of anti-AGE biologics could eventually inform therapeutic strategies for diabetes complications and age-related diseases.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: All computational designs, screening data, binding characterization results, and raw data files will be deposited on Zenodo under a Creative Commons Attribution (CC-BY 4.0) license within 3 months of project completion, regardless of outcome.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: A preregistration will be maintained on ResearchHub, and results will be shared as a preprint on bioRxiv prior to journal submission.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
